meta.title: Cell Organelle Quest
meta.subject: Biology
learning_objectives: Students will identify and describe the functions of major cell organelles.
Students will understand the process of cellular respiration.

environment.skybox_prompt: a futuristic science lab with holographic displays of cellular processes

assets.name: Lysosome
assets.description: Digestive vesicle; breaks down waste at pH 4.5.

assets.name: Glucose
assets.description: C₆H₁₂O₆ fuel molecule for cellular respiration.

assets.name: Cellular Waste
assets.description: Cellular waste debris — must be digested by the lysosome.

assets.name: Cellular Waste
assets.description: Cellular waste debris — must be digested by the lysosome.

assets.name: Cellular Waste
assets.description: Cellular waste debris — must be digested by the lysosome.

assets.name: Protein Vesicle
assets.description: Newly synthesized protein vesicle traveling through the endomembrane system.

phases.title: Welcome to the Cell Organelle Quest!
phases.instruction: Embark on a journey to explore the functions of cell organelles and the process of cellular respiration.

phases.title: Identify the Powerhouse
phases.instruction: Click on the organelle responsible for ATP production.

phases.title: Fuel the Powerhouse
phases.instruction: Drag the glucose molecule to the mitochondria to start cellular respiration.

phases.title: Clean Up the Cell
phases.instruction: Drag all 3 waste particles into the Lysosome to digest them. (0/3)

phases.title: Protein Processing Pathway
phases.instruction: Follow the secretory pathway: drag the protein vesicle to the ER, then to the Golgi Apparatus.

phases.title: Mission Complete!
phases.instruction: You have successfully explored the cell organelles and cellular respiration process!

click-mitochondria.title: Mitochondria
click-mitochondria.body: Mitochondria are known as the powerhouses of the cell. They convert glucose (C₆H₁₂O₆) into ATP, the energy currency of the cell.
click-mitochondria.misconception: Students often confuse mitochondria with chloroplasts, which are only found in plant cells and perform photosynthesis.

drag-glucose.title: Cellular Respiration
drag-glucose.body: During cellular respiration, glucose is broken down with oxygen to produce ATP: C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + 36ATP.

drag-multi-lysosome.title: Lysosome Function
drag-multi-lysosome.body: Lysosomes contain digestive enzymes that break down waste materials and cellular debris at an acidic pH.
drag-multi-lysosome.misconception: Lysosomes are sometimes mistaken for vesicles that transport proteins, but they primarily digest waste.

drag-chain-protein-pathway.title: Protein Pathway
drag-chain-protein-pathway.body: Proteins are synthesized in the endoplasmic reticulum and modified in the Golgi apparatus for transport and use in the cell.

npc.name: Dr. Cell
npc.persona: An enthusiastic and knowledgeable cell biologist with a passion for teaching about cellular functions and processes.

hints.click-mitochondria: Remember, the powerhouse of the cell is responsible for producing energy!
hints.drag-glucose: Think of what fuels the mitochondria to produce energy.
hints.drag-multi-lysosome: Look for parts of the cell that need cleaning.
hints.drag-chain-protein-pathway: Follow the path from protein synthesis to processing.
